objective: Boot 4 bare node types (gateway, broker, compute, schema) on iroh substrate. Day-1 topology UI + day-1 rfa CLI. Zero app logic, just mesh participants. Provable substrate before any feature work.
background: Greenfield rebuild. Custom QUIC mesh permanently retired per Golden Principle #13. iroh is the default substrate candidate; libp2p / quinn+chitchat / quinn+foca are the fallback ladder. Full context in docs/plans/mesh-v1/00-mesh-rebuild-prd.md + 01-substrate-prd.md.
in_scope: 4 bare node binaries (rafka-gateway, rafka-broker, rafka-compute, rafka-schema) — each boots, mints identity, joins mesh, emits substrate spans
rafka-topology-ui web UI on localhost:19090 — live topology graph + spawn/kill buttons (plain HTML+JS, no SPA framework, no node_modules)
rfa CLI binary (crates/rfa/) — mesh node add/remove/list/describe/logs/spans + mesh topology show/watch + mesh status + mesh wait-converged commands
All 14 substrate spans (per docs/plans/mesh-v1/01-substrate-prd.md §9) emit OTLP and land in tests/artifacts/mesh-substrate/
Topology UI joins mesh as view-only node (separate ALPN rafka-topology-v1)
Subprocess management for spawn/kill — UI process owns node lifecycle, CLI calls UI backend (single control surface)
out_of_scope: Kafka wire protocol (api_key handling, SASL, etc.) — post-substrate
Topics, records, SingleWal, schema registration — post-substrate
Chaos primitives + soak runner — Sprint 02 deliverable
Any HTTP routes on broker/compute/schema/gateway beyond what's needed for the UI's localhost:19090 binding
Custom QUIC mesh code path as a fallback — permanently retired (Golden Principle #13)
exit_criteria: cargo run -p rafka-gateway boots a node, mints identity at $RAFKA_DATA_DIR/node-identity.json, joins mesh via iroh, emits rafka.mesh.node.started
Same for rafka-broker, rafka-compute, rafka-schema (4 node types all alive on iroh)
cargo run -p rafka-topology-ui starts UI on http://localhost:19090
Open browser to UI: see empty graph + 'Spawn <type>' buttons
Click 'Spawn gateway' in UI: blue node appears in graph within 5s
Click 'Spawn broker' + 'Spawn compute': nodes appear with edges to gateway within 5s
rfa mesh node add --type schema --name sch-1: orange node appears in UI within 5s
rfa mesh node remove sch-1: node disappears from graph within 10s
Kill any node externally (taskkill / kill -9): UI shows rafka.mesh.peer.staleness_timeout in span timeline within 4×gossip_interval
Restart killed node with SAME identity (same node-identity.json): peers re-establish within 5s
All 14 substrate spans present in tests/artifacts/mesh-substrate/*.spans.jsonl with non-empty content
Zero hand-rolled mesh primitives: grep -rn 'fn.*gossip\|fn.*discover_peer\|fn.*nat_traverse' --include='*.rs' returns ZERO matches outside the iroh dependency
Zero custom QUIC mesh code: grep -rn 'QuicMeshManager\|run_control_plane' --include='*.rs' returns zero matches
rfa mesh wait-converged --timeout 30s exits 0 after spawning 4 nodes
discipline: Sonnet only for every subagent dispatch — set model explicitly
No defer language — full scope in one merge
OTLP artifact evidence before sprint close (CLAUDE.md mandate)
stash → pull --rebase main → commit → push between fix batches
No Claude attribution in commit messages
Workspace gate (cargo check --workspace --tests --no-default-features) zero/zero before every commit
Trust-but-verify (80/20 rule) — all agent reports verified against git diff
One task per agent dispatched at a time
No HTTP routes on any node binary except rafka-topology-ui (per Golden Principle #6)
pre_reads: docs/plans/mesh-v1/00-mesh-rebuild-prd.md (this sprint's PRD, copied to sprint-prd.md)
docs/plans/mesh-v1/01-substrate-prd.md (iroh substrate + 14 span catalog + boot sequence)
docs/plans/mesh-v1/02-topology-ui-prd.md (UI requirements — joins mesh as view-only node, no SPA framework)
docs/plans/mesh-v1/05-sprint-plan.md (full sprint sequence, this is Sprint 0)
docs/plans/mesh-v1/06-decisions.md (18 locked decisions including Golden Principle #13)
iroh docs (https://docs.rs/iroh) — Endpoint, discovery, ALPN, gossip patterns
CLAUDE.md (if exists in the new repo) for testing/observability discipline
acceptance_workflow: 1. Engineer creates branch sprint-01-substrate-spike off main
2. Engineer ships in order: (a) iroh MeshTransport impl, (b) 4 bare node binaries, (c) rafka-topology-ui binary, (d) rfa CLI
3. Each subsystem lands as its own commit with workspace gate passing
4. After all exit criteria GREEN: engineer flips sprint-config.json status to 'closed', sets closes date, single commit
5. Push branch (no PR) and SendMessage team-lead with branch tip SHA + OTLP artifact path
